full_response:
Let the strategy space for each player be   
S_A = S_B = [0,1].  
For a profile (x_A , x_B)

 u_A(x_A ,x_B)=   
  0   if x_A+x_B>1  
  x_A otherwise  

 u_B(x_A ,x_B)=   
  0   if x_A+x_B>1  
  x_B otherwise  

For a Nash equilibrium we need each player to choose a best–response to the other’s choice.

-------------------------------------------------
1.  Best-response correspondence
-------------------------------------------------
Fix Bob’s choice x_B.

• If x_B<1  
 Alice can still keep the total ≤1 by choosing any 0≤x_A≤1−x_B.  
 Her utility on that interval is u_A = x_A, which is strictly increasing.  
 Hence the unique maximiser is  
  BR_A(x_B)= { 1−x_B }.

• If x_B=1  
 Any positive claim by Alice makes the sum >1 and gives her 0,  
 and x_A=0 also gives 0.  
 Thus every x_A∈[0,1] maximises her payoff:  
  BR_A(1)= [0,1].

(The correspondence for Bob is obtained by symmetry.)

 BR_B(x_A)= {1−x_A} if x_A<1,  
 BR_B(1)= [0,1].

-------------------------------------------------
2.  Elimination of non–equilibria
-------------------------------------------------
(i)  x_A+x_B<1.  
 Then x_A ≠ 1−x_B, so x_A∉BR_A(x_B).  
 ⇒ Not a Nash equilibrium.

(ii) x_A+x_B>1.  
 Both players get 0.  
 Because x_B≤1, Alice can move to x_A'=1−x_B (< x_A) and obtain u_A=x_A'>0,  
 so (x_A ,x_B) is not an equilibrium.  
 (Same argument for Bob.)

-------------------------------------------------
3.  Profiles with x_A+x_B = 1
-------------------------------------------------
Let x_A+x_B=1.

• If x_B<1 (equivalently x_A<1) we have  
 x_A = 1−x_B ⇒ x_A∈BR_A(x_B),  
 x_B = 1−x_A ⇒ x_B∈BR_B(x_A).  

• If x_B=1 (thus x_A=0) then  
 x_A=0 ∈ BR_A(1) and x_B=1 = 1−0 ∈ BR_B(0).  

• If x_A=1 (thus x_B=0) the argument is symmetric.

Hence every profile with x_A+x_B=1 is a mutual best response, i.e. a Nash equilibrium.

-------------------------------------------------
4.  Completeness
-------------------------------------------------
From parts (i) and (ii) no profile with total bandwidth different from 1 can be a Nash equilibrium, so the set found in part (iii) is the entire set of pure Nash equilibria.

\boxed{\text{All pure Nash equilibria are the strategy pairs }(x_A,x_B)\text{ with }x_A+x_B=1,\; 0\le x_A,x_B\le 1.}

(Geometrically, the whole line segment joining (1,0) and (0,1) in the unit square.)

Confidence: 79%
